title: Comparatives and superlatives
explanation:
Comparing things

Comparative (two things): tall → taller than; big → bigger than; good → better than.

Superlative (the most): tall → the tallest; good → the best.

Short adjectives: -er / -est. Long adjectives: more / the most.

questions.text: My brother is [*taller|tallest|more tall] than me.
This is [*the best|better|good] pizza in town.
She is [*the youngest|younger|more young] student in the class.

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 2
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option for each gap.

questions.text: A Ferrari is ____ than a Fiat. | *more expensive | most expensive | expensiver
Mount Everest is ____ mountain in the world. | *the highest | higher | the higher
My room is ____ than yours. | *smaller | smallest | the smaller
This exercise is ____ than the last one. | *harder | hardest | more hard

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 3
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option to complete the sentences.

questions.text: Tokyo is one of [*the biggest|bigger|biggest] cities in the world.
This film is [*more interesting|interestinger|most interesting] than the book.
She runs [*faster|fastest|more fast] than her brother.
It was [*the worst|worse|bad] day of my life.
